reasoning: FIDE updates its official ratings and rankings only once a month, on the 1st of the month. The Wikipedia page 'FIDE_rankings' reflects this monthly official list. As evidence, the Wikipedia fetch tool showed the page currently reads "The top 20 players were ranked on 1 March 2026 as follows:". Because no new FIDE ranking list will be released between March 15 and March 22, the Wikipedia page will remain unchanged (barring immediate vandalism which admins would revert). Therefore, Wesley So's ranking on March 22 will be exactly the same as his ranking on March 15 (9th). Being perfectly equal satisfies the condition of having a ranking "as high or higher". Additionally, even if live ratings were somehow considered, Wesley So just won the 2026 American Cup in early March, gaining rating points, so his real-time standing is also stable or improving. I cap my forecast at 95% due to platform limits and the minute risk of edge cases like unresolved vandalism on the exact resolution check-time.
